ddls:
CREATE TABLE "container_resources" (
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resource usage record */,
  "container_id" INTEGER /* ID of the container */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* CPU usage of the container (in %) */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage of the container (in MB) */,
  "network_rx" INTEGER /* Network receive bytes */,
  "network_tx" INTEGER /* Network transmit bytes */,
  "disk_read" INTEGER /* Disk read bytes */,
  "disk_write" INTEGER /* Disk write bytes */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the resource usage was recorded */,
  "cpu_limit" REAL /* CPU limit for the container (in %) */,
  "memory_limit" REAL /* Memory limit for the container (in MB) */,
  "disk_limit" INTEGER /* Disk limit for the container (in MB) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resource_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "container_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event */,
  "container_id" INTEGER /* ID of the container */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of event (e.g., create, delete, update) */,
  "event_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the event */,
  "details" TEXT /* JSON object containing additional details about the event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who triggered the event (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_container_events_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "nodes" (
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each node */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the node */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the node */,
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* ID of the cluster where the node belongs */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the node was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the node */,
  "deleted_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the node was deleted (soft delete) */,
  "cpu_total" REAL /* Total CPU capacity of the node (in cores) */,
  "memory_total" REAL /* Total memory capacity of the node (in MB) */,
  "disk_total" INTEGER /* Total disk capacity of the node (in MB) */,
  "os" TEXT /* Operating system of the node */,
  "kernel_version" TEXT /* Kernel version of the node */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "clusters" (
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cluster */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the cluster */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the cluster */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the cluster was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the cluster */,
  "deleted_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the cluster was deleted (soft delete) */,
  "kubernetes_version" TEXT /* Kubernetes version (if applicable) */,
  "control_plane_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the control plane */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cluster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed) */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "auth_method" TEXT /* Authentication method used (e.g., username/password, token, certificate) */,
  "token" TEXT /* Token for token-based authentication */,
  "certificate" TEXT /* Certificate for certificate-based authentication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "container_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric record */,
  "container_id" INTEGER /* ID of the container */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., cpu_usage, memory_usage) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "metric_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the metric (e.g., %, MB, bytes) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "container_id" INTEGER /* ID of the container */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., cpu_usage, memory_usage) */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for the alert */,
  "enabled" INTEGER /* Whether the alert is enabled */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message to be sent when the alert is triggered */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., info, warning, critical) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., email, slack) */,
  "enabled" INTEGER /* Whether the notification is enabled */,
  "notification_message" TEXT /* Message to be sent in the notification */,
  "notification_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the notification (e.g., info, warning, critical) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "container_labels" (
  "label_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each label */,
  "container_id" INTEGER /* ID of the container */,
  "label_key" TEXT /* Key of the label */,
  "label_value" TEXT /* Value of the label */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the label was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the label */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("label_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "container_env" (
  "env_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each environment variable */,
  "container_id" INTEGER /* ID of the container */,
  "env_key" TEXT /* Key of the environment variable */,
  "env_value" TEXT /* Value of the environment variable */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the environment variable was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the environment variable */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("env_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "container_dependencies" (
  "dependency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dependency */,
  "container_id" INTEGER /* ID of the container */,
  "dependency_name" TEXT /* Name of the dependency */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the dependency was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the dependency */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dependency_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "container_config" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration */,
  "container_id" INTEGER /* ID of the container */,
  "config_key" TEXT /* Key of the configuration */,
  "config_value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the configuration was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the configuration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id")
)
